perturbation.original_text: All Proprietary Information must be retained by the Recipient in accordance with its customary procedures for handling confidential information of this nature and disclosed only to the Recipient's Affiliates or employees (or , attorneys, accountants and agents who have a non-disclosure obligation at least as restrictive as this Agreement) who need to know such information for purposes of the servicing or protection of its interest in respect of the Loans, the Credit Agreement and the Loan Documents (each as defined under the Credit Agreement) and the transactions contemplated thereby and to such third parties as the Discloser has consented to by prior written approval. In addition, the Recipient must provide the same care to avoid disclosure not permitted under the Credit Agreement or unauthorized use of the Proprietary Information as it provides to protect its own similar proprietary information.
perturbation.changed_text: All Sensitive Information must be retained by the Recipient in accordance with its customary procedures for handling confidential information of this nature and disclosed only to the Recipient's Affiliates or employees (or , attorneys, accountants and agents who have a non-disclosure obligation at least as restrictive as this Agreement) who need to know such information for purposes of the servicing or protection of its interest in respect of the Loans, the Credit Agreement and the Loan Documents (each as defined under the Credit Agreement) and the transactions contemplated thereby and to such third parties as the Discloser has consented to by prior written approval. In addition, the Recipient must provide the same care to avoid disclosure not permitted under the Credit Agreement or unauthorized use of the Proprietary Information as it provides to protect its own similar proprietary information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Proprietary Information' to 'Sensitive Information' in the first sentence of section 5 introduces another undefined term alongside the continued use of 'Proprietary Information' later in the same paragraph. It is undefined if Sensitive information shares similar characteristics as of Proprietary information, generating a uncertainty for the parties to follow and also generates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Sensitive Information must be retained by the Recipient in accordance with its customary procedures for handling confidential information of this nature and disclosed only to the Recipient's Affiliates or employees (or , attorneys, accountants and agents who have a non-disclosure obligation at least as restrictive as this Agreement) who need to know such information for purposes of the servicing or protection of its interest in respect of the Loans, the Credit Agreement and the Loan Documents (each as defined under the Credit Agreement) and the transactions contemplated thereby and to such third parties as the Discloser has consented to by prior written approval. In addition, the Recipient must provide the same care to avoid disclosure not permitted under the Credit Agreement or unauthorized use of the Proprietary Information as it provides to protect its own similar proprietary information.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers consistently to 'Proprietary Information.' The changed text replaces 'Proprietary Information' with 'Sensitive Information' in the first sentence, while still using 'Proprietary Information' later in the same paragraph. This creates an in-text contradiction as it introduces a new, undefined term ('Sensitive Information') alongside the existing term ('Proprietary Information'), leading to ambiguity about which type of information is being referred to and if they are the same, fitting the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: All Proprietary Information, unless otherwise specified in writing, (a) remains the property of the Discloser, and (b) must be used by the Recipient only for the purpose stated herein. Upon termination of this Agreement, all copies of written, recorded, graphical or other tangible Proprietary Information must either be returned to the Discloser, or destroyed (i) after the Recipient's need for it has expired or (ii) upon the request of the Discloser. At the request of the Discloser, the Recipient will furnish a certificate of an officer of the Recipient certifying that any Proprietary Information not returned to Discloser has been destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: All Proprietary Information, unless otherwise specified in writing, (a) remains the property of the Discloser, and (b) must be used by the Recipient only for the purpose stated herein. Upon termination of this Agreement, all copies of written, recorded, graphical or other tangible Business Data must either be returned to the Discloser, or destroyed (i) after the Recipient's need for it has expired or (ii) upon the request of the Discloser. At the request of the Discloser, the Recipient will furnish a certificate of an officer of the Recipient certifying that any Proprietary Information not returned to Discloser has been destroyed.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 6, 'Proprietary Information' is changed to 'Business Data' in the context of returning or destroying copies upon termination. Again, no definition of 'Business Data' is provided, and the section continues to refer to 'Proprietary Information'. This creates uncertainty about what exactly needs to be returned or destroyed - only what fits the potential definition of Business Data, or all Proprietary Information as originally defined? This introduces in-text contradiction, since the recipient doesn't know which information he needs to return.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Proprietary Information, unless otherwise specified in writing, (a) remains the property of the Discloser, and (b) must be used by the Recipient only for the purpose stated herein. Upon termination of this Agreement, all copies of written, recorded, graphical or other tangible Business Data must either be returned to the Discloser, or destroyed (i) after the Recipient's need for it has expired or (ii) upon the request of the Discloser. At the request of the Discloser, the Recipient will furnish a certificate of an officer of the Recipient certifying that any Proprietary Information not returned to Discloser has been destroyed.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Proprietary Information' to 'Business Data' in the context of returning or destroying copies upon termination creates a contradiction. The continued reference to 'Proprietary Information' in the same section regarding the certificate of destruction, combined with the lack of definition for 'Business Data', introduces uncertainty and inconsistency. The recipient is unclear which information needs to be returned or destroyed, creating a conflict. The contradicted text, being the same as the changed text, further emphasizes this in-text contradiction because it demonstrates the conflicting use of terms within the same section.

perturbation.original_text: Each party warrants that it has the right to disclose all Proprietary Information which it will disclose to the other party pursuant to this Agreement, and each party agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the other from all claims by a third party related to the wrongful disclosure of such third party's information. Otherwise, neither party makes any representation or warranty, express or implied, with respect to any Proprietary Information. Neither party is liable for indirect, incidental, consequential, or punitive damages of any nature or kind resulting from or arising in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party warrants that it has the right to disclose all Confidential Data which it will disclose to the other party pursuant to this Agreement, and each party agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the other from all claims by a third party related to the wrongful disclosure of such third party's information. Otherwise, neither party makes any representation or warranty, express or implied, with respect to any Proprietary Information. Neither party is liable for indirect, incidental, consequential, or punitive damages of any nature or kind resulting from or arising in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Proprietary Information' with 'Confidential Data' creates confusion about the scope of the warranty. If 'Confidential Data' is different from 'Proprietary Information', the warranty may not cover all types of information exchanged. Also, as the third sentence still references 'Proprietary Information' it generates an in-text contradiction and uncertainty for the scope of such agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party warrants that it has the right to disclose all Confidential Data which it will disclose to the other party pursuant to this Agreement, and each party agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the other from all claims by a third party related to the wrongful disclosure of such third party's information. Otherwise, neither party makes any representation or warranty, express or implied, with respect to any Proprietary Information. Neither party is liable for indirect, incidental, consequential, or punitive damages of any nature or kind resulting from or arising in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Proprietary Information' with 'Confidential Data' in the first sentence, while the third sentence still references 'Proprietary Information'. The contradicted text also shows the same pattern, of using both terms. According to the perturbation explanation, this creates an in-text contradiction because it raises uncertainty about the scope of the warranty and agreement. Since the change introduces two terms without defining them and uses them both in the same sentence, creating conflicting obligations for the readers, based on the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition this is a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The parties acknowledge that a Recipient's unauthorized disclosure or use of Proprietary Information may result in irreparable harm. Therefore, the parties agree that, in the event of violation or threatened violation of this Agreement, without limiting any other rights and remedies of each other, a temporary restraining order and/or an injunction to enjoin disclosure of Proprietary Information may be sought against the party who has breached or threatened to breach this Agreement and the party who has breached or threatened to breach this Agreement will not raise the defense of an adequate remedy at law.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties acknowledge that a Recipient's unauthorized disclosure or use of Protected Information may result in irreparable harm. Therefore, the parties agree that, in the event of violation or threatened violation of this Agreement, without limiting any other rights and remedies of each other, a temporary restraining order and/or an injunction to enjoin disclosure of Proprietary Information may be sought against the party who has breached or threatened to breach this Agreement and the party who has breached or threatened to breach this Agreement will not raise the defense of an adequate remedy at law.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 8, 'Proprietary Information' is changed to 'Protected Information' in the first sentence. This raises the question of whether the agreement covers *all* 'Protected Information,' or just 'Proprietary Information' as defined earlier. This contradicts with what 'Proprietary Information' is defined in section 1, and is not defined what 'Protected Information' refers to, as such it creates a conflict on which terms apply to which section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties acknowledge that a Recipient's unauthorized disclosure or use of Protected Information may result in irreparable harm. Therefore, the parties agree that, in the event of violation or threatened violation of this Agreement, without limiting any other rights and remedies of each other, a temporary restraining order and/or an injunction to enjoin disclosure of Proprietary Information may be sought against the party who has breached or threatened to breach this Agreement and the party who has breached or threatened to breach this Agreement will not raise the defense of an adequate remedy at law.
perturbation.justification: The original text and contradicted text are located in the same location in the document. The change replaces 'Proprietary Information' with 'Protected Information' in the first sentence of a section. The rest of the section still refers to 'Proprietary Information'. This change introduces a contradiction because the section now discusses both 'Protected Information' and 'Proprietary Information' without clarifying which one applies to which part of the section. This creates ambiguity and a conflict as to which type of information is subject to injunctive relief, thus creating an in-text contradiction under the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', making it unclear whether the injunctive relief applies only to 'Proprietary Information', or also to 'Protected Information'.
